root_text.ud6.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud6.6:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā nānātitthiyasamaṇabrāhmaṇaparibbājakā sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasanti nānādiṭṭhikā nānākhantikā nānārucikā nānādiṭṭhinissayanissitā.
root_text.ud6.6:2.2: “sassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:3.2: “asassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:4.2: “sassato ca asassato ca attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:5.2: “neva sassato nāsassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:6.2: “sayaṅkato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:7.2: “paraṅkato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:8.2: “sayaṅkato ca paraṅkato ca attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:9.2: “asayaṅkāro aparaṅkāro adhiccasamuppanno attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:10.2: “sassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:11.2: “asassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:12.2: “sassatañca asassatañca sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:13.2: “neva sassataṁ nāsassataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:14.2: “sayaṅkataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:15.2: “paraṅkataṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:16.2: “sayaṅkatañca paraṅkatañca sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:17.2: “asayaṅkāraṁ aparaṅkāraṁ adhiccasamuppannaṁ sukhadukkhaṁ attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti.
root_text.ud6.6:18.1: Te bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.6:19.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.6:19.2: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.6:19.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud6.6:20.1: “Idha, bhante, sambahulā nānātitthiyasamaṇabrāhmaṇaparibbājakā sāvatthiyaṁ paṭivasanti nānādiṭṭhikā nānākhantikā nānārucikā nānādiṭṭhinissayanissitā.
root_text.ud6.6:21.2: ‘sassato attā ca loko ca, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti …pe…
root_text.ud6.6:21.3: te bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.6:22.2: Te atthaṁ na jānanti anatthaṁ na jānanti, dhammaṁ na jānanti adhammaṁ na jānanti.
root_text.ud6.6:22.3: Te atthaṁ ajānantā anatthaṁ ajānantā, dhammaṁ ajānantā adhammaṁ ajānantā bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ mukhasattīhi vitudantā viharanti:
root_text.ud6.6:23.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud6.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud6.6:1.3: Now at that time several ascetics, brahmins, and wanderers who followed various other religions were residing in Sāvatthī, holding different views, beliefs, and opinions, relying on different views.
translation_text.ud6.6:2.1: There were some ascetics and brahmins who had this doctrine and view:
translation_text.ud6.6:2.2: “The self and the cosmos are eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile.”
translation_text.ud6.6:3.1: Others held views such as the following, each regarding their own view as true and others as silly.
translation_text.ud6.6:3.2: “The self and the cosmos are not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.6:4.2: “The self and the cosmos are both eternal and not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.6:5.2: “The self and the cosmos are neither eternal nor not eternal.”
translation_text.ud6.6:6.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by oneself.”
translation_text.ud6.6:7.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by another.”
translation_text.ud6.6:8.2: “The self and the cosmos are made by both oneself and another.”
translation_text.ud6.6:9.2: “The self and the cosmos have arisen anomalously, not made by oneself or another.”
translation_text.ud6.6:10.2: “Pleasure and pain are eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:11.2: “Pleasure and pain are not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:12.2: “Pleasure and pain are both eternal and not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:13.2: “Pleasure and pain are neither eternal nor not eternal, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:14.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by oneself, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:15.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:16.2: “Pleasure and pain are made by both oneself and another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:17.2: “Pleasure and pain have arisen anomalously, not made by oneself or another, and the self and the cosmos.”
translation_text.ud6.6:18.1: They were fighting, quarreling, and disputing, continually wounding each other with barbed words:
translation_text.ud6.6:18.3: Such is not Truth, such is Truth!”
translation_text.ud6.6:19.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud6.6:19.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what was happening.
translation_text.ud6.6:23.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud6.6:24.2: which is tied up with the other-maker.
translation_text.ud6.6:24.3: There are some who do not realize this,
translation_text.ud6.6:24.4: they do not see the dart.
translation_text.ud6.6:25.1: But when they see this dart,
translation_text.ud6.6:25.2: they do not think, ‘I make it’,
translation_text.ud6.6:26.1: These folk are caught up in conceit,
translation_text.ud6.6:26.2: tied by conceit, shackled by conceit.
